name: Tropius

attacks.name: Fruity Aroma
attacks.text: Look at the top 6 cards of your deck, and you may reveal any number of Pokémon you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: Delicious fruits grew out from around its neck because it always ate the same kind of fruit.

name: Grubbin

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It spits sticky threads and winds them around branches, then swings nimbly from tree to tree in a pendulum-like motion.

name: Fomantis

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Many Trainers give their Fomantis their own flowerpots so they can sunbathe in peace and quiet.

name: Lurantis ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Lively Cutter
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 200 more damage.

attacks.name: Leaf Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Poltchageist

abilities.name: Hide 'n' Sneak
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks and Abilities done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)

attacks.name: Furtive Drop
attacks.text: Place 1 damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Poltchageist looks like a regional form of Sinistea, but it was recently discovered that the two Pokémon are entirely unrelated.

name: Sinistcha

abilities.name: Hide 'n' Sneak
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks and Abilities done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)

attacks.name: Matcha Spin
attacks.text: If you have 6 or more Pokémon that have the Hide 'n' Sneak Ability in your discard pile, place 4 damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: It prefers cool, dark places, such as the back of a shelf or the space beneath a home's floorboards. It wanders in search of prey after sunset.

name: Heatran

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Lava Wall
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Burned Pokémon.

flavorText: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross- shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

name: Mega Delphox ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Trick Portal
attacks.text: Look at the top 9 cards of your deck, and you may put any number of Pokémon you find there onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

attacks.name: Eerie Glow
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

name: Sizzlipede

attacks.name: Controlled Burn
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Bug Out
attacks.text: Reveal the bottom 7 cards of your deck, and this attack does 50 damage for each Pokémon you find there that has the Bug Out attack. Then, shuffle any revealed Pokémon back into your deck. Discard the other cards.

flavorText: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

name: Centiskorch

attacks.name: Controlled Burn
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

name: Charcadet

attacks.name: Best Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its firepower increases when it fights, reaching over 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit. It likes berries that are rich in fat.

name: Armarouge

attacks.name: Flame Legion
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any Fire Energy attached.

flavorText: This Pokémon clads itself in armor that has been fortified by psychic and fire energy, and it shoots blazing fireballs.

name: Goldeen
flavorText: Its dorsal, pectoral, and tail fins wave elegantly in water. That is why it is known as the Water Dancer.

name: Seaking

attacks.name: Hydro Jet
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: In autumn, its body becomes more fatty in preparing to propose to a mate. It takes on beautiful colors.

name: Wailmer
flavorText: It shows off by spraying jets of seawater from the nostrils above its eyes. It eats a solid ton of Wishiwashi every day.

name: Wailord ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Falling Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Relicanth

attacks.name: Fossil Beatdown
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has "Antique" in its name.

flavorText: Rock-hard scales and oil-filled swim bladders allow this Pokémon to survive the intense water pressure of the deep sea.

name: Popplio
flavorText: The balloons it inflates with its nose grow larger and larger as it practices day by day.

name: Brionne
flavorText: It cares deeply for its companions. When its Trainer is feeling down, it performs a cheery dance to try to help.

name: Primarina

abilities.name: Enriching Melody
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aqua Return
attacks.text: Shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

flavorText: Also known as a songstress, it is a sight to behold on moonlit nights when it sings in front of the colony it leads.

name: Finizen

attacks.name: Draining Fin
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its water ring is made from seawater mixed with a sticky fluid that Finizen secretes from its blowhole.

name: Palafin

attacks.name: Knuckle Justice
attacks.text: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 200 more damage.

flavorText: This hero of the ocean swims at a speed of 50 knots and saves drowning people and Pokémon.

name: Electrike

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. This Pokémon's whole body gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

name: Manectric

attacks.name: Flashing Barrier
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Evolution Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sonic Edge
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It discharges electricity from its mane. The Pokémon creates a thundercloud overhead from which to drop lightning bolts.

name: Charjabug
flavorText: As it digests fallen leaves, it generates and stores electricity, which it can discharge from the tips of its jaws.

name: Vikavolt

attacks.name: Quick Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Giga Railgun
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has no Voltaic Lightning Energy attached, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: When carrying a Charjabug, Vikavolt can receive electricity from it and then rapidly fire powerful electromagnetic beams from its large jaws.

name: Mega Zeraora ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Thunderous Fist
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage for each Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Zepto Turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Miraidon

abilities.name: Photon Cord
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, move up to 2 Basic Lightning Energy cards from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: This seems to be the Iron Serpent mentioned in an old book. The Iron Serpent is said to have turned the land to ash with its lightning.

name: Slowpoke

attacks.name: All-You-Can-Yeet
attacks.text: You may discard any number of cards from your hand.

flavorText: It lazes vacantly near water. If something bites its tail, it won't even notice for a whole day.

name: Slowbro

attacks.name: All Out
attacks.text: If you have no cards in your hand, this attack does 160 more damage.

flavorText: An attached Shellder won't let go because of the tasty flavor that oozes out of Slowbro's tail.

name: Mega Slowbro ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shellnado Spin
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 12 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

name: Jynx

attacks.name: Intense Kiss
attacks.text: At the end of your opponent's next turn, discard the Defending Pokémon and all attached cards.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

name: Shuppet

abilities.name: Hide 'n' Sneak
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks and Abilities done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)

flavorText: It uses its horn to feed on envy and malice-or so it's said. It's very active at night.

name: Banette

abilities.name: Hide 'n' Sneak
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks and Abilities done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)

attacks.name: Puppet Pull
attacks.text: You may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: A doll bore a grudge over being junked, and it became a Pokémon. It seeks the child that disowned it.

name: Spiritomb

attacks.name: Spiritual End
attacks.text: If you have 13 or more Pokémon that have the Hide 'n' Sneak Ability in your discard pile, choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon and quadruple the number of damage counters on each of them.

flavorText: It was formed by uniting 108 spirits. It has been bound to the Odd Keystone to keep it from doing any mischief.

name: Litwick
flavorText: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel that it burns.

name: Lampent

attacks.name: Spreading Light
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Lampent and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Lampent appears at the moment of death and promptly absorbs the spirit as it leaves the body.

name: Mega Chandelure ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Binding Flame
abilities.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

attacks.name: Phantom Maze
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

name: Dhelmise

attacks.name: Vengeful Anchor
attacks.text: If you have 4 or more Pokémon that have the Hide 'n' Sneak Ability in your discard pile, this attack does 140 more damage.

flavorText: After a piece of seaweed merged with debris from a sunken ship, it was reborn as this ghost Pokémon.

name: Marshadow

attacks.name: Shadowy Knot
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: By slipping into the shadow of a martial arts master and copying their movements, this Pokémon learned the ultimate techniques.

name: Annihilape

abilities.name: Durable Body
abilities.text: If this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

attacks.name: Ghostly Blow
attacks.text: Place 5 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It imbues its fists with the power of the rage that it kept hidden in its heart. Opponents struck by these imbued fists will be shattered to their core.

name: Primeape
flavorText: Some researchers theorize that Primeape remains angry even when inside a Poké Ball.

name: Cranidos

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: Cranidos toughen up their already sturdy heads by headbutting one another.

name: Rampardos ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Destructive Headbutting
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may use this Ability. Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rowdy Hammer
attacks.text: During your next turn, attacks used by this Pokémon do 150 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Battle Claw
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Gaia Impact
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Mega Darkrai ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dusk Raid
attacks.text: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 110 more damage.

attacks.name: Abyss Eye
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, it is Knocked Out.

name: Vullaby
flavorText: Its healthy appetite leads to visible growth spurts. It often has to replace the bones it wears as its size increases.

name: Mandibuzz

attacks.name: Bone Sniper
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any Special Energy attached. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Watching from the sky, they attack weakened prey on the ground. They have a habit of decorating themselves with bones.

name: Inkay

attacks.name: Procurement
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It drains foes' will to fight by flashing light at them. It then takes the opportunity to hide itself away.

name: Malamar

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Brain Crush
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon isn't Confused, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It wields the most compelling hypnotic powers of any Pokémon, and it forces others to do whatever it wants.

name: Nickit
flavorText: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokemon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

name: Thievul

attacks.name: Skill Thief
attacks.text: If you have no cards in your hand, choose an attack from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon in play and use it as this attack.

flavorText: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

name: Morpeko ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wheely Draw
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.

attacks.name: Hangry Blaster
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

name: Zarude

attacks.name: Overhead Throw
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Shadowy Whip
attacks.text: If your Benched Pokémon have any Shadowy Darkness Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Within dense forests, this Pokemon lives in a pack with others of its kind. It's incredibly aggressive, and the other Pokemon of the forest fear it.

name: Maschiff
flavorText: Its well-developed jaw and fangs are strong enough to crunch through boulders, and its thick fat makes for an excellent defense.

name: Mabosstiff

attacks.name: Plunging Headbutt
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Mabosstiff loves playing with children. Though usually gentle, it takes on an intimidating look when protecting its family.

name: Chi-Yu

attacks.name: Whirling Envy
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has 2 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 90 more damage. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

flavorText: The envy accumulated within curved beads that sparked multiple conflicts has clad itself in fire and become a Pokémon.

name: Skarmory

attacks.name: Steel Cutter
attacks.text: Discard up to 2 Basic Metal Energy cards from your hand, and this attack does 40 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: Because it nests in bramble bushes, the feathers of its chicks grow hard from being scratched by thorns.

name: Shieldon

attacks.name: Smithereen Smash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon lived in primeval jungles. It's thought that Shieldon had few enemies thanks to its sturdy face.

name: Bastiodon

abilities.name: Ancient Bulwark
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to each of your Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that have 2 or less Energy attached.

flavorText: Bastiodon live in herds. When assaulted by enemies, they line up side by side and use their hard faces to block attacks.

name: Bronzor

attacks.name: Mirror Attack
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Metal Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: They are found in ancient tombs. The patterns on their backs are said to be imbued with mysterious power.

name: Bronzong

attacks.name: Metal Block
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks from Evolution Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It brought rains by opening portals to another world. It was revered as a bringer of plentiful harvests.

name: Mega Excadrill ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Undermine
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Maximum Drilling
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 130 more damage.

name: Pikipek

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Pikipek has strong muscles in its neck, so it won't hurt itself even if it violently shakes its head.

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

name: Toucannon

abilities.name: Aerial Draw
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Draw a card.

attacks.name: Feather Rondo
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: Pairs of Toucannon are considered symbols of companionship, as these Pokémon will raise the temperature of their beaks to warm each other.

name: Type: Null
flavorText: It was modeled after a mighty Pokémon of myth. The mask placed upon it limits its power in order to keep it under control.

name: Silvally

abilities.name: Call a Buddy
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if you have no cards in your hand, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The final factor needed to release this Pokémon's true power was a strong bond with a Trainer it trusts.

name: Bombirdier

attacks.name: Challenging Delivery
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, search your deck for a Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.

name: Antique Armor Fossil
rules:
Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic Colorless Pokémon. This card can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat.
 
At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.

abilities.name: Protective Armor
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, all of your Pokémon take 10 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Antique Skull Fossil
rules:
Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic Colorless Pokémon. This card can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat.
 
At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.

abilities.name: Spiny Skull
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

name: Backtrack Badge
rules: Once during your turn, after you flip any coins for an attack of the Colorless Pokémon this card is attached to, you may ignore all results of those coin flips and begin flipping those coins again.

name: Dark Bell
rules: Both Active non-Darkness Pokémon are now Confused.

name: Fossil Quarry
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may search their deck for up to 2 Item cards that have "Antique" in their name and put them onto their Bench. Then, that player shuffles their deck.

name: Gladion's Final Battle
rules:
You can use this card only when it is the last card in your hand.
During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon that don't have a Rule Box do 80 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V_atk, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

name: Gwynn
rules: Discard up to 2 Pokémon that don't have a Rule Box from your hand, and draw 3 cards for each card you discarded in this way. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V_atk, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

name: Jett
rules: Draw a card for each of your opponent's Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play.

name: Misty's Vitality
rules: Search your deck for up to 4 Basic Water Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.

name: Rust Syndicate Grunt
rules:
You can use this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.

Discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

name: Tremendous Bomb
rules: If the Pokémon this card is attached to isn't a Mega Evolution Pokémon ex, is in the Active Spot, and takes 240 or more damage from an attack from your opponent's Mega Evolution Pokémon ex (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 12 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon. If you placed any damage counters in this way, discard this card.

name: Shadowy Darkness Energy
rules:
As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Darkness Energy.
 
As long as the Darkness Pokémon this card is attached to is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to it by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

name: Voltaic Lightning Energy
rules:
As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Lightning Energy.
 
Attacks used by the Lightning Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Fomantis

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Many Trainers give their Fomantis their own flowerpots so they can sunbathe in peace and quiet.

attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any Fire Energy attached.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Evolution Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sonic Edge
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: .It discharges electricity from its mane. The Pokémon creates a thundercloud overhead from which to drop lightning bolts.

attacks.text: If you have no cards in your hand, this attack does 160 more damage.

flavorText: An attached Shellder won't let go because of the tasty flavor that oozes out of Slowbro's tail.

attacks.text: If you have 4 or more Pokémon that have the Hide 'n' Sneak Ability in your discard pile, this attack does 140 more damage.

flavorText: After a piece of seaweed merged with debris from a "sunken ship, it was reborn as this ghost Pokémon.

flavorText: Bastiodon live in herds. When assaulted by enemies, they line up side by side and use their hard faces to block attacks.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Draw a card.

attacks.name: Feather Rondo

flavorText: Pairs of Toucannon are considered symbols of companionship, as these Pokémon will raise the temperature of their beaks to warm each other.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The final factor needed to release this Pokémon's true power was a strong bond with a Trainer it trusts.

attacks.name: Leaf Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage for each Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Zepto Turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Binding Flame
abilities.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

attacks.name: Phantom Maze
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Destructive Headbutting
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may use this Ability. Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rowdy Hammer
attacks.text: During your next turn, attacks used by this Pokémon do 150 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dusk Raid
attacks.text: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 110 more damage.

attacks.name: Abyss Eye
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, it is Knocked Out.

name: Morpeko ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wheely Draw
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.

attacks.name: Hangry Blaster
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

name: Mega Excadrill ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Undermine
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Maximum Drilling
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 130 more damage.

name: Brave Bangle
rules: If the Pokémon this card is attached to doesn't have a Rule Box, the attacks it uses do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V_atk, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

name: Crushing Hammer
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

name: Dark Bell
rules: Both Active non-Darkness Pokémon are now Confused.

name: Energy Switch
rules: Move a Basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

name: Gladion's Final Battle
rules:
You can use this card only when it is the last card in your hand.
During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon that don't have a Rule Box do 80 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V_atk, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

name: Gwynn
rules: Discard up to 2 Pokémon that don't have a Rule Box from your hand, and draw 3 cards for each card you discarded in this way. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V_atk, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

name: Iron Defender
rules: During your opponent's next turn, all of your Metal Pokémon take 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (This includes new Pokémon that come into play.)

name: Misty's Vitality
rules: Search your deck for up to 4 Basic Water Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.

name: Rust Syndicate Grunt
rules:
You can use this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.

Discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

name: Tremendous Bomb
rules: If the Pokémon this card is attached to isn't a Mega Evolution Pokémon ex, is in the Active Spot, and takes 240 or more damage from an attack from your opponent's Mega Evolution Pokémon ex (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), place 12 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon. If you placed any damage counters in this way, discard this card.

name: Mega Zeraora ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Thunderous Fist
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage for each Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Zepto Turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.name: Binding Flame
abilities.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

attacks.name: Phantom Maze
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

name: Mega Darkrai ex
rules: Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.
